title: ostrya virginiana subsp. guatemalensis
intro: Ostrya virginiana subsp. guatemalensis, also known as Guatemalan Hop-hornbeam, is a subspecies of the Hop-hornbeam tree, native to the tropical regions of Central America. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The Guatemalan Hop-hornbeam has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and fight infections. The plant can be prepared as a tea or infusion, or used in tincture or decoction form, making it a versatile herbal remedy for promoting overall well-being.
